---
title: "Matilda tutorial 3 — Dimension reduction"
output: html_document
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

Encode each cell's RNA + ADT + ATAC features into a single integrated latent
space, stored in `reducedDim(., "MATILDA")`.

```{r setup}
source("_setup.R")
ref <- if (file.exists("matilda_teaseq_ref.rds")) readRDS("matilda_teaseq_ref.rds") else
  matilda_train(load_teaseq_sce("train"), label = "cell_type")
```

## Reduce

```{r reduce}
ref <- matilda_reduce(ref)
dim(reducedDim(ref, "MATILDA"))
```

## Visualise

```{r plot, fig.width = 6, fig.height = 5}
L <- reducedDim(ref, "MATILDA")
plot(L[, 1], L[, 2], col = factor(ref$cell_type), pch = 16, cex = 0.4,
     xlab = "latent 1", ylab = "latent 2", main = "Matilda latent space")
# For a UMAP instead:
#   ref <- scater::runUMAP(ref, dimred = "MATILDA")
#   scater::plotUMAP(ref, colour_by = "cell_type")
```

## Performance validation

The integrated latent space is **bit-identical** to the original Matilda Python
implementation (max|Δ| = 0). See `inst/scripts/parity_check.R`.

Next: [feature selection](04-feature-selection.Rmd).
